name: JPMorgan Private Bank and Wealth Management
scope_note: Covers J.P. Morgan Wealth Management (branch-based advisors, mass affluent to HNW) and J.P. Morgan Private Bank (UHNW, $10M+ clients) only. Excludes JPMorgan Chase consumer banking, commercial banking, investment banking, and J.P. Morgan Asset Management divisions.
ai_strategy_summary: JPMorgan's AI strategy in wealth management and private banking centres on three live deployments: Connect Coach for advisors (90% faster sourcing of discussion materials), Smart Monitor for investors (83% less research time), and Guidelines for operations (+50% productivity). AI tools are cited as a key driver of 20% gross sales growth in 2023–2024. Harvard Business School concluded JPMorgan is 'playing an entirely different game.'
headline_metric: $6T AWM client assets — Connect Coach 90% faster discussion sourcing, 20% gross sales growth YoY
headline_initiative: Connect Coach (advisors, 90% faster), Smart Monitor (investors, 83% faster research), Guidelines (operations, +50% productivity) — three production AI platforms disclosed at 2025 Investor Day

key_metrics.client_assets_note: Year-end 2024, per 2025 Investor Day slide 3. AWM AUS split roughly equally between AM and GPB.
key_metrics.advisors_gpb_note: GPB client advisors as of 2024, per Investor Day slide 3 — 1.6x growth since 2014.
key_metrics.advisors_cwm_note: Chase Wealth Management advisors, per Investor Day slide 8.
key_metrics.advisors_hni_pb_note: Advisers serving high-net-worth private bank clients, per Reuters/HBS case study (May 2025).

advisor_productivity.headline: Connect Coach: 90% faster sourcing of advisor discussion materials — tailored outreach, meeting prep, real-time insights (Investor Day slide 12)
advisor_productivity.detail: Connect Coach — deployed for JPMorgan advisors — provides tailored outreach ideas, meeting prep and next steps, real-time market insights, and answers from internal experts. Disclosed at the 2025 Investor Day as delivering a 90% reduction in time to source discussion materials (before vs. today). JPMorgan CIO Mike Urciuoli separately stated advisors find information 'up to 95% faster' (Reuters, May 2025). The division cited AI tools as a key driver of 20% gross sales growth (2023–2024). Forward target: advisors expand client rosters by 50% over 3–5 years.
evidence: Connect Coach: 90% reduction in time to source discussion materials — Investor Day slide 12 (verified from source)
Mike Urciuoli (CIO): advisors find information 'up to 95% faster' — Reuters, May 5, 2025 (verbatim quote)
20% YoY gross sales growth 2023–2024 — Reuters, attributed to JPMorgan; not in Investor Day deck
50% more clients per adviser target over 3–5 years — Reuters, May 5, 2025
4,000 advisers serving HNW private bank clients — Reuters/HBS case study context

sources.name: JPMorgan 2025 Investor Day — Asset & Wealth Management (slide 12)
Reuters — JPMorgan says AI helped boost sales (Urciuoli/Erdoes quotes)

client_personalization.headline: AI pre-populates real-time market data and client investment preferences — enabling tailored advice at scale during volatility
client_personalization.detail: JPMorgan AWM CEO Mary Erdoes described the personalisation capability during spring 2025 market volatility: 'When you have a tool that pre-populates all the data and the movement in real time, while also remembering clients' old investment preferences and helps in tailoring a plan for them quickly, it also allows advisors to do much more.' Connect Coach (Investor Day slide 12) explicitly lists 'tailored outreach ideas' as a core feature, and 'proactive and anticipatory client advice' was added as a NEW AI use case for 2025.
evidence: Mary Erdoes (CEO, AWM): AI pre-populates market movement data + client investment preferences in real time — Reuters, May 5, 2025 (verbatim quote)
Connect Coach feature: 'tailored outreach ideas' — Investor Day slide 12
'Proactive and anticipatory client advice' listed as NEW 2025 AI use case — Investor Day slide 12

sources.name: Reuters — JPMorgan says AI helped boost sales (Erdoes quote)
JPMorgan 2025 Investor Day — Asset & Wealth Management (slide 12)

investment_portfolio.headline: AI-informed model portfolios announced — spanning public and private markets across the WM advisor network
investment_portfolio.detail: J.P. Morgan Wealth Management offers AI-informed model portfolios spanning public equities, fixed income, and alternative/private market asset classes. Advisors use these as a starting framework, with AI surfacing allocation suggestions aligned to client risk profiles and market conditions. Investment decisions remain human-approved — AI informs the recommendation, not the final decision.
evidence: Model portfolios spanning public equities, fixed income, and alternatives referenced in WM platform materials
AI-assisted allocation described in investor presentations — specific product not separately announced
Human advisor approval required; AI role in portfolio construction not independently confirmed

sources.name: J.P. Morgan 2025 Investor Day — Asset & Wealth Management (PDF)

research_content.headline: Smart Monitor (Spectrum) cuts investor research time by 83% — AI workflow, automated insights, custom universes
research_content.detail: Smart Monitor — built on JPMorgan's Spectrum platform — is deployed for investors and portfolio teams. It provides AI-integrated workflow, automated insights, custom investment universes, and extensive market coverage. The 2025 Investor Day disclosed an 83% reduction in time spent researching a topic (before vs. today). The tool sits alongside Connect Coach (advisors) and Guidelines (operations) as one of three production AI platforms confirmed at Investor Day.
evidence: Smart Monitor (Spectrum): 83% reduction in time spent researching a topic — Investor Day slide 12 (verified from source PDF)
Features: AI-integrated workflow, automated insights, custom universes, extensive coverage — Investor Day slide 12
Deployed for investors/portfolio teams alongside Connect Coach and Guidelines

sources.name: JPMorgan 2025 Investor Day — Asset & Wealth Management (slide 12)
